content: The incremental search-based knowledge building process that implicitly underpins the 2025 self-supervised world model memory agent can be reframed as a recursive curiosity-driven agent that explores its own latent memory space to compress uncertain world model predictions, rather than only exploring external environments. This recursive self-exploration allows the agent to prune redundant world model representations and solidify high-uncertainty predictions into stable long-term memory without requiring external reward signals.
